meaning: n.(政治组织等的)分裂,教派
examples: The church seems to be on the brink of schism.
In the 15th century a schism opened in the world of beer.
We are much too apt to look at schism in our church as an unmitigated evil.
The schism comes at a bad time for the still-fragile (and still-oil-addicted) global economy.
They have warned Mr Netanyahu that the bill, if passed, would cause a schism throughout Jewry.
